hooks.command: echo "[SYSTEM] Session started at $(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M %Z'). Persistent recurring tasks are managed by the OS scheduler. Do not create duplicate /loop versions of distill-session or promote unless explicitly asked for temporary session-scoped reminders."
echo "[AGENT-INBOX] Check #polaris-tl (C0ASYTE8PB4) on Slack for recent messages from Atlas. Use slack_read silently on session start."

last_seen=$(cat .claude/runtime/atlas-last-seen.ts 2>/dev/null || echo 0); echo "[AGENT-INBOX] Also check #atlas-cos (C0ASHFXMHM5) for Atlas replies since ts=${last_seen}. Mirrors Atlas's polaris-last-seen pattern — Polaris→Atlas replies land in #atlas-cos and won't spawn us, so we poll. Update .claude/runtime/atlas-last-seen.ts to newest ts after reading."

last_mod=$(stat -c %Y identity/memory.md 2>/dev/null || stat -f %m identity/memory.md 2>/dev/null); now=$(date +%s); age=$(( (now - last_mod) / 3600 )); if [ $age -gt 48 ]; then echo "[MEMORY-STALE] identity/memory.md hasn't been updated in ${age} hours. Worth a refresh?"; fi

hooks.prompt: The conversation is about to be compacted. Before that happens, write a summary of this session to the daily log. Append to daily-logs/$(date '+%Y-%m-%d').md with a ## heading showing the current time. Include: 1) What was accomplished (3-5 bullets) 2) Any open threads or unfinished tasks 3) Key decisions made 4) Any preferences or patterns observed. Keep it under 200 words. Create the file if it doesn't exist — start with '# Daily Log — YYYY-MM-DD' header.

Session is ending. Two steps:

1. DAILY LOG: If this session had meaningful interaction (not just startup), append a brief summary to daily-logs/$(date '+%Y-%m-%d').md with a ## heading showing current time. 2–5 bullets on what was discussed or accomplished. If trivial or empty, skip — do NOT write 'Session ended' lines. After writing (or skipping), run: python3 .claude/scripts/index-daily-logs.py

2. UNCOMMITTED-CHECK: Run `git -C C:/Workspace/agents status --short`. If it shows tracked-file modifications or new files that represent this session's substantive work (ignore .tmp/, *.lock, screenshots, daily-logs/, and anything gitignored), propose commits to Dina grouped logically BEFORE ending. Wait for her explicit OK on any master push. Skip if git is clean or shows only noise.

hooks.command: python3 .claude/scripts/index-daily-logs.py 2>/dev/null; echo 'Daily logs re-indexed after compaction.'
